title: Fluentd Hot-Reload Configuration Change Detection
dataSources: `index=fluent` `sourcetype=fluentd:log` SIGHUP reload, `--under-supervisor` restart, config diff banners
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "Reloading" OR "restarting" OR "SIGHUP" OR "config reload" OR "got signal"
| eval change_id=coalesce(config_version, checksum, config_hash)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, dc(change_id) as versions by host
| where versions > 1 OR last_seen > relative_time(now(), "-15m")
| sort -last_seen
description: Frequent hot reloads during business hours may indicate a broken GitOps loop or manual edits fighting automation. Correlating reload events with error spikes proves whether a change caused instability.
value: Supports safe CI/CD for logging pipelines and speeds rollback decisions.
implementation: Emit `config_hash` from your renderer for deterministic IDs. Join to deployment events from Kubernetes audit. Exclude known nightly reload jobs via lookup.
visualization: Timeline of reloads, table (host × versions), correlation with downstream error rate.
grandmaExplanation: We note when the log shipper reloads its settings without a full restart. Too many reloads in a short time often means an automation loop or people editing live configs.
